current_phase.label: Host boundary and autonomous development process foundation

current_milestone.label: Create standalone platform repo and codify anti-drift workflow gates

progress_summary: Standalone platform repository has been created and pushed to GitHub at git@github.com:zhaohernando-code/ai-control-platform.git to prevent platform-core work from drifting into managed business projects. Existing Chinese design docs, capability matrix, recovery redesign, and PC/mobile visual drafts are copied into this repo. Host-boundary and project-onboarding gates are active. Current-session platform materials have been migrated into this repo: formal contracts and visuals are active docs, while stock_dashboard autonomous-flow trials/code/tests and root hook route fixes are quarantined under docs/migrations and legacy for platform-neutral refactoring. The current round extended the platform self-trial from text evaluation into machine-readable workflow primitives: run manifest, artifact ledger, task DAG, goal guard, LLM reviewer gate, model router, workbench projection, autonomous continuation, process hardening, operator event ingestion, workbench snapshot publishing, and the first PC/mobile workbench shell. The reviewer gate is now a platform data model; Claude+DeepSeek is treated as one provider/model option selected by policy, not a hard-coded workflow. Workbench projection now provides PC/mobile one-screen state input without requiring UI code to parse logs. Projection schema validation and CLI gate protect PC/mobile rendering from malformed projection JSON. Autonomous continuation now prevents the platform from stopping after a completed cycle while PROJECT_STATUS.next_step or next_work_packages are available and no human blocker exists. PC and mobile workbench shells are separate static entries and consume validated projection JSON. Workbench projection source abstraction supports a service endpoint through a safe query parameter while preserving the local fixture fallback. A lightweight local workbench server now exposes projection and projection-history APIs, and browser verification confirms desktop/mobile API-backed rendering has no horizontal overflow. Projection history selection now lets operators switch between current and previous autonomous cycles in both desktop and mobile shells. Workbench controls now persist operator action events through /api/workbench/events, so validate/next actions become durable process facts instead of UI-only text changes. Process-hardening is now connected to executable closeout via npm run check:process-hardening and npm run check:closeout, browser event verification is committed as npm run check:workbench:browser-events, and run-with-node18 makes closeout self-heal from the current Node 16 shell by selecting an available Node 18+ runtime. Operator events can now be ingested into Run Manifest events and Artifact Ledger artifacts idempotently and atomically, so workbench actions participate in autonomous evaluation state instead of remaining a side-channel ledger. Workbench projection generation now applies operator event ingestion before summarizing manifest, artifacts, autonomous_run, and closeout evidence, ignores stale run_result/run_evaluation whenever operator events are present, and browser closeout now covers both desktop event writes and mobile projection rendering. Workbench server now prefers projection history input_path snapshots and dynamically generates projection from workflow state, with projection_path retained as historical fallback. History item paths are constrained to relative docs/examples or snapshot paths, and the server has tests proving dynamic input priority over static fallback. Snapshot APIs can now persist projection-ready workflow state input and update projection history latest. Autonomous continuation now emits a snapshot_publish_plan only when workflow_state passes the same publish readiness gate used by the publisher; otherwise it returns snapshot_publish_issues. Closeout runner now executes that plan through either local snapshot publishing or the workbench snapshot API, refuses non-platform local roots, rejects output paths outside the platform repo, validates HTTP API acknowledgements and returned projection identity, rejects unknown modes, normalizes snapshot ids, fails closed when the plan is missing, writes success/failure evidence back to workflow_state manifest events plus artifact ledger, and republishes the evidence-bearing workflow_state to the same snapshot id so workbench latest can display it. PC/mobile projection schemas now require a closeout summary object, both workbench shells render closeout status from projection.closeout, deterministic loop coverage proves decideContinuation -> runCloseoutPlan -> projection -> decideContinuation remains autonomous, runAutonomousCloseoutLoop exposes that path as a reusable orchestration runner plus CLI, and the CLI can write replayable autonomous-closeout-loop-run.v1 input/output artifacts. Replay validation now checks stored autonomous closeout loop artifacts before reuse, including host identity, result consistency, projection schema, closeout evidence, and next continuation state. Scheduler continuation reuse now goes through prepareAutonomousContinuationFromLoopArtifact and run-autonomous-closeout-loop --resume-from, so invalid replay artifacts become structured blockers instead of continuation input. Blocked replay validation attempts with available workflow state now write autonomous_loop_replay_validation events and failed evaluation artifacts back into workflow_state for workbench projection and recovery evaluation. Model routing now supports codex_plan_pressure and adds DeepSeek V4 Pro process_guard before expensive GPT implementation/arbitration work. Reviewer gate now creates DeepSeek invocation policies with official Anthropic endpoint, Claude Code model, keepalive expectations, bounded timeout profiles, effort, scope limits, split_required signals, and provider smoke recovery so DS Pro can participate more often without turning timeout into a manual blocker. Workbench projection and PC/mobile shells now expose replay validation blockers as first-class resume_health status instead of burying them in artifact totals. Reviewer provider health facts now persist smoke, retry, split, and fallback decisions into manifest events and artifact ledger, and PC/mobile workbench projection exposes latest provider health and next action. Reviewer tool timeout recovery now has a concrete reviewer_scope_split layer: smoke-pass DS tool timeouts can be converted into durable bounded shards, continuation consumes those shards as run_reviewer_scope_shard work packages, and PC/mobile projection exposes shard_count, pending_shards, and next_shard. Reviewer shard execution is now durable: shard results and aggregates are persisted as manifest/artifact facts, continuation skips completed shards, aggregate findings feed manifest.review_findings after all shards complete, and PC/mobile projection exposes reviewer_shard_review status. Reviewer shard outputs now have reusable recording paths: a CLI, workbench API, and projection-source method can persist shard results and optional aggregates without manual JSON edits. Reviewer shard execution now has a provider-neutral runner that selects pending shards, builds read-only prompts, requires an executor adapter, records shard results, and aggregates automatically after the final shard. A Claude+DeepSeek shard executor adapter now uses the canonical claude-deepseek skill wrapper and no-proxy launcher path, parses structured findings, and converts timeouts or wrapper failures into durable reviewer findings. Reviewer shard runner now has a scheduler CLI entrypoint, npm run run:reviewer-shard, that executes pending shards with the Claude+DeepSeek adapter by default or deterministic mock findings in tests, writes workflow state, and aggregates after the final shard.
next_step: Run a real bounded DS shard through npm run run:reviewer-shard against a temporary workflow snapshot, capture timeout/success facts, and harden provider-health integration for runner-level failures.
